normalized_spec.summary: A mobile survival typing game where letters spawn around the player and move inward. The player types matching letters to destroy them before they reach the player. The goal is to survive as long as possible.
normalized_spec.core_loop: Enemy letter spawns, enemy moves toward player, player types matching letter, matching enemy is destroyed, more letters continue spawning, survive as long as possible.
normalized_spec.player_fantasy: The player feels like they are defending themselves by typing quickly and accurately under pressure.

rules: letters typed must match enemy letter to destroy it

normalized_spec.fail_condition: A letter enemy reaches the player.
normalized_spec.difficulty_model: Difficulty increases over time by shorter spawn intervals, slightly faster average movement, more overlapping pressure. For M1 only basic speed variation: slow letters, fast letters.

scenes.name: Main Menu
scenes.purpose: Start screen with play button and title.

scenes.name: Gameplay
scenes.purpose: Active gameplay where letters spawn and player types.

scenes.name: Fail / Result Popup
scenes.purpose: Show survival time, retry and menu options.

layout_notes: Gameplay must reserve bottom screen space for the mobile keyboard.
HUD should not overlap important enemy movement area.
Fail popup should clearly show end of run and retry option.
Typed letter matching should feel obvious.

entities.name: Enemy Letter
entities.role: spawns around player, moves inward, has speed (slow/fast), may duplicate letters

out_of_scope: advanced enemy types beyond basic speed variation
acceptance_criteria: screen layout works with mobile keyboard
survival loop is understandable within seconds
players want to retry for a better survival time

validation_checks.description: Verify that MainMenu.tscn, Gameplay.tscn, and FailPopup.tscn exist in the scenes folder.
Verify that all listed script files exist in the scripts folder.
Check that Gameplay scene contains nodes: Player, EnemySpawner, HUD, ScoreManager, DifficultyScaler.
Ensure Player node has a script that connects to InputEvent and processes typed characters.
Verify EnemySpawner script emits a signal or directly instantiates EnemyLetter nodes.
Confirm HUD contains a Label node that updates survival time.

repair_hints: If a scene file is missing, create it with the specified root node type and name.
If a script file is missing, create an empty GDScript file at the specified path.
Ensure that the Player script connects to the InputEvent and checks typed characters against child EnemyLetter nodes.
Add a Timer node to EnemySpawner to control spawn intervals.
Add a Timer or process function in DifficultyScaler to adjust spawn rate over time.
